books.title: Tales the Totems Tell
books.author: Hugh Weatherby
books.rationale: This excerpt contains the story of the first totem pole and the most common totem figure, the raven.
books.noteText: PDF provided for member use only under Fair Use Copyright Law.

books.title: Frozen Land
books.author: Jan Reynolds
books.rationale: Kenalogak, a young girl, is part of the Inuit culture in Canada. How do people survive in such a cold climate? Watch as Kenalogak's family finds food and makes shelter and clothing for their survival.
books.noteText: Used briefly. You may wish to check out from the library.

books.title: The Very First Americans
books.author: Cara Ashrose & Bryna Waldman
books.rationale: This book gives a brief overview of how humans came to North America and how the very diverse people groups lived.
books.noteText: Available on Learning Ally with Audio.
books.scopeText:
Term 1 (Partial Term)
Term 2 (Partial Term)
Term 3 (Partial Term)

books.title: The Book of Indians
books.author: Holling C. Holling
books.rationale: Little Turtle lives on the cliffs of the desert. The other children make fun of him because he is slow and clumsy, so he plays by himself. One day, strangers approach Little Turtle's village. What Little Turtle does in response makes him a hero.
books.noteText: PDF provided for member use only under Fair Use Copyright Law.

books.title: Moonstick: The Seasons of the Sioux
books.author: Eve Bunting
books.rationale: A young boy comes of age under the thirteen moons of the Sioux year. With each notch in his father's moon-counting stick, the boy marvels at the world around him, observing the sometimes subtle, sometimes remarkable, changes in the seasons and in his own tribe's way of living.
books.noteText: Used briefly. You may wish to check out from the library.

books.title: Itse Selu: Cherokee Harvest Festival
books.author: Daniel Pennington
books.rationale: A captivating glimpse of Cherokee life in pre-Columbian North America. Join Little Wolf's family as they prepare to celebrate Itse Selu, the harvest festival. As night approaches the village, the magic of anticipation fills the air. The luscious feast, Grandfather's storytelling, and the sacred corn dance weave a magical tapestry of tradition.
books.noteText: Used briefly. You may wish to check out from the library.

books.title: Hiawatha and the Peacemaker
books.author: Robbie Robertson
books.rationale: This is the traditional story of how Hiawatha brought rival tribes together to defeat the evil Tadodaho and form the powerful Iroquois Nation in the 14th century.
books.noteText: Some images may be scary for sensitive children. Used briefly. You may wish to check out from the library. Available on Learning Ally with Audio.
books.sharedText:
History: Grade 1 (U.S.)
History: Grade 1 (Canada)

books.title: The Honour Drum
books.author: Cheryl Bear and Tim Huff
books.rationale: Written as a partnership between two friends--an Indigenous woman and a non-Indigenous man--this story introduces children to the history, stories, traditions, and symbols that have been meaningful to Indigenous people from the distant past right up to today. Its aim is to foster understanding, empathy, and appreciation, and to spark meaningful conversations with your children.

books.title: The Pencil
books.author: Susan Avingaq
books.rationale: In The Pencil, the Inuk filmmaker and author Susan Avingaq recalls childhood memories from her early life in the Arctic, living in an igloo with her family. Through this joyful story, Avingaq shows the importance of good stewardship, using what we have wisely, and the delight that can be found in simple objects.

books.title: Thirteen Moons on Turtle's Back: A Native American Year of Moons
books.author: Joseph Bruchac & Jonathan London
books.rationale: In Native American legend, the thirteen scales on a turtle's back symbolize the annual thirteen cycles of the moon and the seasonal changes each one brings. The thirteen poems in this book -- one for each moon of the year, and each from a different indigenous group -- celebrate the natural world and weave together traditional knowledge and folklore. Each poem is illustrated by a beautiful painting.
books.scopeText:
Term 1 (Partial Term)
Term 2 (Partial Term)
Term 3 (Partial Term)

books.title: The Gift of the Great Buffalo
books.author: Carole Lindstrom
books.rationale: Through the story of Rose, a Métis-Ojibwe girl on the prairie in the 1860s, The Gift of the Buffalo introduces young readers to Métis culture and the vital relationship between Indigenous communities and the buffalo, which provided sustenance and shelter.

books.title: Maple Moon
books.author: Connie Brummel Crook
books.rationale: In Maple Moon, Connie Brummel Crook borrows from traditional folk tales to create an enchanting fictional account of how maple syrup might have been discovered. The protagonist, a young Mississauga boy named Rides the Wind, learns of the sweet water because his physical limitations prevent him from joining a hunt; yet, it is this discovery that helps his community survive the winter. Illustrated by beautiful paintings, this book teaches children that strength can come in many forms.

books.title: On the Trapline
books.author: David A. Robertson
books.rationale: This is an award-winning picture book, which tells the gentle story of a boy accompanying his grandfather on a trip back to his childhood home where he and his family lived off the land.

books.title: Hiawatha and the Peacemaker
books.author: Robbie Robertson
books.rationale: This is the traditional story of how Hiawatha brought rival tribes together to defeat the evil Tadodaho and form the powerful Iroquois Nation in the 14th century.
books.noteText: Some images may be scary for sensitive children. Used briefly. You may wish to check out from the library. Available on Learning Ally with Audio.
books.sharedText:
History: Grade 1 (U.S.)
History: Grade 1 (Canada)

books.title: Abraham Lincoln: An Initial Biography
books.author: Genevieve Foster
books.rationale: This classic biography is back in print! Foster presents the life of Abraham Lincoln through an engaging narrative that starts with his humble beginnings and takes the reader through his political rise, his presidency during the Civil War, and his assassination and legacy.
books.noteText: Please use the Option 1 link to utilize the discount code.

books.title: United States Atlas 7th Ed.
books.author: National Geographic Kids
books.rationale: This atlas is used across the curriculum as a reference and as a text to read in some lessons. With interesting information and pictures, this is an economical reference for students.
books.noteText: Optional for Grade 1. For U.S. students.
books.maySubText: earlier edition. Other atlases may be used; however, map work and other lessons refer specifically to this one.
books.sharedText:
Geography: Grade 1
Geography: Grade 7 (U.S.)
Resources: Grade 9
Resources: Grade 10
Resources: Grade 11
Resources: Grade 12
Geography: Grade 2 (U.S.)
Geography: Grade 3 (U.S.)
Geography: Grade 8 (U.S.)
History: Grades 2-3 (U.S.)

books.title: Escape by Night: A Civil War Adventure
books.author: Laurie Myers
books.rationale: This historical fiction book does a great job of introducing slavery and the Civil War to younger students. The main character, Tommy, who lives in the South meets a wounded Union soldier, which brings the realities of opposing sides in the war into personal perspective. Tommy also questions the institution of slavery and his own views on justice and humanity. Another interesting aspect of the book is that the wounded soldier has a commonplace book and shares quotes with Tommy.

books.title: Coolies
books.author: Yin
books.rationale: The experience of Chinese immigrants is captured in this book. During the mid-1800s thousands of Chinese workers came to America to build the Transcontinental Railroad. This book, which was inspired by actual events, is the story of two Chinese brothers who come to California to make a better life for themselves and their families back home. They find work helping to build the Transcontinental Railroad and find that life in America is much more difficult than they thought it would be.
books.noteText: Used briefly. You may wish to check out from the library. Available on Learning Ally with Audio.

books.title: Dandelions
books.author: Eve Bunting
books.rationale: This longer-length picture book helps students understand westward expansion through the eyes of a pioneer family. The story shows what it was like to leave a familiar home, travel west, and settle on the Great Plains, highlighting the emotional and physical challenges families faced.
books.noteText: Used briefly. You may wish to check out from the library.

books.title: Buffalo Bird Girl: A Hidatsa Story
books.author: S. D. Nelson
books.rationale: This book lets students walk in the shoes of a Native American girl. It is based on the real childhood experiences of a Hidatsa woman born around 1839 who lived along the Missouri River on the Great Plains. Students are introduced to the tribe's customs and daily routines, their relationships with other tribes, and their initial interactions with the white man. The story also tells how the Hidatsa way of life was changed forever when they were forced to relocate to a reservation.

books.title: The Spirit of Canada: Canada's Story in Legends, Fiction, Poems, and Songs
books.author: Barbara Hehner
books.rationale: Organized thematically, this beautiful anthology encompasses a wide variety of voices and stories that reflect Canada's past and present. It is an excellent accompaniment to Canadian history and literature, as it contains many different genres: poems, songs, letters, legends, stories, folk tales, historical accounts, and more.
books.noteText: Canadian students should purchase this book and NOT American Tall Tales for the Tall Tales/Fables course.
books.sharedText:
Canadian History: Grades 2-4
Tall Tales/ Fables

books.title: The Kids Book of Canadian History
books.author: Carlotta Hacker
books.rationale: This book brings Canada's history to life with engaging illustrations, helpful maps, and a narrative that keeps the complex events of our nation's past straightforward enough for even the youngest historians.
books.scopeText:
Term 1 (Partial Term)
Term 2 (Partial Term)
Term 3 (Partial Term)

books.title: Laura Secord's Brave Walk
books.author: Connie Brummel Crook
books.rationale: Laura Secord's Brave Walk introduces young readers to the War of 1812 through the courage of one Canadian, who embarked on a long journey through a forest at night to deliver a message to the British troops. The engaging narrative and illustrations bring this episode of Canadian history to life for young readers.

books.title: Belle of Batoche
books.author: Jacqueline Guest
books.rationale: Belle of Batoche is a work of historical fiction which imagines one girl's experience in the Battle of Batoche in 1885, where Métis fighters led by Louis Riel raised an ultimately unsuccessful defence against Canadian militia troops in Batoche, Saskatchewan. A simple story of two girls' rivalry and eventual friendship is woven into an historically-accurate depiction both of life in the late-nineteenth century Canadian prairies and of specific events from the battle itself.
books.noteText: This is good as an independent read for Grade 4 or confident readers.

books.title: Born!: A Foal, Five Kittens and Confederation
books.author: Deirdre Kessler
books.rationale: A delightful story about the everyday life of twin siblings in Charlottetown during the Charlottetown Conference of 1864. Readers will learn about everyday life in nineteenth-century British North America as well as the events and political figures who were key to establishing Canada's Confederation.

books.title: I Am Not a Ghost: The Canadian Pacific Railway
books.author: David Bouchard
books.rationale: This beautifully-illustrated book tells the story of Chinese railway workers whose contributions to the building of the Canadian Pacific Railway were essential, yet who faced racism, hardships, exploitation, and even death. At the heart of this story is friendship and hope, when one gravely ill Chinese man is saved and cared for by Amelia Douglas, a Métis woman who was the wife of the first governor of British Columbia, as well as a trained nurse and midwife.

books.title: SeaMan: The Dog Who Explored The West With Lewis & Clark
books.author: Gail Langer Karwoski
books.rationale: The exciting adventures of Lewis and Clark are captured in this engaging book. This fictional account is full of accurate details drawn from Lewis’s own diary entries. This book provides students an interesting and accessible way to understand the challenges, dangers, and daily life of the Lewis and Clark expedition.

books.title: Lincoln: A Photobiography
books.author: Russell Freedman
books.rationale: This book follows the events leading up to, during, and after the Civil War through the life of Abraham Lincoln.  As students read about Lincoln’s personal and public struggles, students will learn about the character and leadership of this man during one of the most tumultuous times of our nation’s history. This is the first war during which photography was used to show the people back home what war was really like, so the images capture not only the events, but also the new technology of the age.
books.noteText: Available on Learning Ally with Audio.

books.title: Children of the Wild West
books.author: Russell Freedman
books.rationale: The author does an excellent job of showing what it was like for pioneer children to experience life in the 1800's. Many fascinating photographs are included that help students see how different life was back then. He also tells about what life was like for some Native American children who had to navigate boarding schools and give up their traditional ways.
books.noteText: Available with Audio on Learning Ally.

books.title: Heart and Soul: The Story of America and African Americans
books.author: Kadir Nelson
books.rationale: This book is written in the voice of an old African American grandmother taking a child on her knee to tell the stories of the black experience throughout American history. A couple of chapters are read each term. It will also be used the following year.
books.noteText: Available on Learning Ally with Audio.

books.title: William Wilberforce: The Freedom Fighter
books.author: Derick Bingham
books.rationale: This biography tells about the man who dedicated his life to ending the slave trade and slavery in the British Empire. The book follows the life of William Wilberforce (1759-1833) beginning with his days as a school boy to being a member in Parliament who risked being thought a fool for standing by his principles. Students will not only learn about this man of God but also learn about what was happening in England during this time period.

books.title: Under the Hawthorn Tree
books.author: Marita Conlon-McKenna
books.rationale: This book is a fictional account of a family during the 1840s when Ireland was devastated by the potato famine. Although it is a dark time in history, this story also shows the resilience of the three siblings, who continue to care for one another despite hunger, fear, and constant uncertainty. This is an important event that helps students understand why so many Irish immigrants came to the US during this time period.

books.title: Commodore Perry in the Land of the Shogun
books.author: Rhoda Blumberg
books.rationale: During this period in history, Japan had been isolated from the outside world for centuries. But when American Commodore Perry and his troops sailed to Japan with a letter from the President, everything began to change. This Newberry Honor book is an engaging way for students to explore global history, diplomacy, and cultural change through a well-told true story.

books.title: The Spirit of Canada: Canada's Story in Legends, Fiction, Poems, and Songs
books.author: Barbara Hehner
books.rationale: Organized thematically, this beautiful anthology encompasses a wide variety of voices and stories that reflect Canada's past and present. It is an excellent accompaniment to Canadian history and literature, as it contains many different genres: poems, songs, letters, legends, stories, folk tales, historical accounts, and more.
books.noteText: Canadian students should purchase this book and NOT American Tall Tales for the Tall Tales/Fables course.
books.sharedText:
Canadian History: Grades 2-4
Tall Tales/ Fables

books.author: Carlotta Hacker

books.author: Derick Bingham

The book follows the life of William Wilberforce (1759-1833) beginning with his days as a school boy to being a member in Parliament who risked being thought a fool for standing by his principles. Students will not only learn about this man of God but also learn about what was happening in England during this time period.

Although it is a dark time in history, this story also shows the resilience of the three siblings, who continue to care for one another despite hunger, fear, and constant uncertainty. books.author: Rhoda Blumberg
books.rationale: During this period in history, Japan had been isolated from the outside world for centuries. But when American Commodore Perry and his troops sailed to Japan with a letter from the President, everything began to change. This Newberry Honor book is an engaging way for students to explore global history, diplomacy, and cultural change through a well-told true story.

books.title: The Student Bible Atlas, Revised Edition
books.author: Tim Dowley
books.rationale: This is a simple, inexpensive Bible atlas that provides full-page maps of the biblical lands of the Old and New Testaments. In Bible lessons, students will be directed to look at specific pages. This resource is for Grades 3-8 ONLY.
books.maySubText: other Bible atlas, though page numbers in lesson plans will not match.
books.sharedText:
Old Testament: Grades 3-6
New Testament: Grades 3-6
New Testament: Grades 7-8
Old Testament: Grades 7-12
Story of Arithmetic: Part 1
Story of Geometry: Part 1
Ancient History: Grades 5-6

books.title: The Bronze Bow
books.author: Elizabeth George Speare
books.rationale: Students will be immersed in the world of first-century Judea as they read this Newberry Award Winner. This book helps students understand the historical realities of Roman occupation and Jewish resistance. It tells the story of 18 year old Daniel bar Jamin, who seeks to avenge his father’s death and is filled with hatred toward the Romans who are beginning to invade his country of Israel. Through the friendships he makes and the words he hears spoken by Jesus of Nazareth, Daniel learns that hatred is not the answer.
books.noteText: Available on Learning Ally with Audio.

books.title: Redemption: The Church in Ancient Times
books.author: Luke H. Davis
books.rationale: Students will learn about Christians from this time period whose faith led many of them to martyrdom.  Beginning with Peter at Pentecost, the author tells about saints such as Ignatius, Cyprian and Athanasius who defended the truths of Christianity against heresy. Instead of just giving us facts about the different people, the author tells a story about a time in their life that helps the reader understand who they are and why their story is recorded in history. This volume is a great way for students to see how these important church fathers fit into the stream of history. These were the life and times they were living— when the persecution from the Roman Empire was prevalent.

books.title: Joe and the Wreck of the Tribune
books.author: Jacqueline Halsey
books.rationale: At the turn of the nineteenth century, a thirteen-year old boy risked his life to save drowning sailors from the wreck of the H.M.S. Tribune, off the coast of Nova Scotia. This historical fiction novel imagines a backstory for the boy only known to history as "Joe Cracker," and it paints a detailed picture of what life was like in a small fishing village around 1800.

books.title: The Story of Canada
books.author: Janet Lunn, Christopher Moore
books.rationale: Canadian history is told through rich narrative, recreations of daily life, folk tales, and fascinating facts.
books.maySubText: previous edition. Page numbers will differ from lesson plans.
books.scopeText:
Term 1 (Partial Term)
Term 2 (Partial Term)
Term 3 (Partial Term)
books.sharedText:
Canadian History: Grades 5-6
Canadian History: Grades 7-8

books.title: The Loxleys and Confederation
books.author: Mark Zuehlke
books.rationale: The story of Canada's Confederation of 1867 told in a graphic novel format. Students meet key historical figures whose debates and vision shaped the course of Canada's history. The story also introduces other significant events during the period, and includes a written afterward that serves as a historical overview.

books.title: Tecumseh
books.author: James Laxer
books.rationale: This book personalizes the events of the War of 1812 by presenting it through the life and experiences of Tecumseh, a Shawnee chief and close friend and ally of Major General Isaac Brock. Written by a historian of nineteenth-century Canada and biographer of Tecumseh, this book introduces readers to the important role played by Indigenous groups in the wars that shaped Canada.

books.title: William Wilberforce: The Freedom Fighter
books.author: Derick Bingham
books.rationale: This biography tells about the man who dedicated his life to ending the slave trade and slavery in the British Empire. The book follows the life of William Wilberforce (1759-1833) beginning with his days as a school boy to being a member in Parliament who risked being thought a fool for standing by his principles. Students will not only learn about this man of God but also learn about what was happening in England during this time period.

Through the friendships he makes and the words he hears spoken by Jesus of Nazareth, Daniel learns that hatred is not the answer.
books.noteText: Available on Learning Ally with Audio.

books.title: Redemption: The Church in Ancient Times
books.author: Luke H. Davis
books.rationale: Students will learn about Christians from this time period whose faith led many of them to martyrdom.  Beginning with Peter at Pentecost, the author tells about saints such as Ignatius, Cyprian and Athanasius who defended the truths of Christianity against heresy. Instead of just giving us facts about the different people, the author tells a story about a time in their life that helps the reader understand who they are and why their story is recorded in history. This volume is a great way for students to see how these important church fathers fit into the stream of history. These were the life and times they were living— when the persecution from the Roman Empire was prevalent.

books.title: Sacajawea
books.author: Joseph Bruchac
books.rationale: By blending careful historical research with storytelling, Bruchac presents the Lewis and Clark expedition through an Indigenous perspective. This novel helps students see Sacajawea as a complex, intelligent young woman whose language skills, cultural knowledge, and resilience were essential to the expedition’s survival.
books.noteText: Available on Learning Ally with Audio.

books.title: A History of US: Liberty for All?: 1820-1860
books.author: Joy Hakim
books.rationale: This volume is a great spine that covers the times and events between 1820-1860. Through the use of engaging narrative, photographs and primary sources, Hakim helps students get a feel for the US during a time of westward expansion, railroad building, and industrialization.
books.noteText: Available on Learning Ally with Audio.

books.title: Abraham Lincoln and Frederick Douglass: The Story Behind and American Friendship
books.author: Russell Freedman
books.rationale: This book explores the Civil War era through the evolving relationship of these two highly influential men. It is a fascinating look into how real people grappled with the moral issues of the day.
books.noteText: Available on Learning Ally with Audio.

books.title: Gold Mountain
books.author: Betty G. Yee
books.rationale: This historical fiction book introduces students to the Chinese immigrant experience in the 1800’s when thousands of Chinese workers came to the US to work on the Transcontinental Railroad. It is the story of a young Chinese girl who disguises herself as a boy and takes her deceased brother’s contract to work for the Central Pacific railroad. This book helps students get a better understanding of Chinese culture, the hardships and discrimination Chinese people faced, and the hard work it took to create the transcontinental railroad.

books.title: A Year in the South: 1865
books.author: Stephen Ash
books.rationale: This true, nonfiction account uses letters and diaries to tell the stories of four different people over the course of 1865–both during and right after the Civil War ends. It gives the student a look into the lives of people who were maneuvering through a tumultuous change. It encourages students to see that Reconstruction was a complex time that didn’t mean instant equality or peace.
books.noteText: The students will not read all four stories.

books.title: In the Shadow of the Alamo
books.author: Sherry Garland
books.rationale: By reading In the Shadow of the Alamo, students will be able to step into the shoes of a fifteen-year-old boy named Lorenzo Bonifacio who is plucked up by his government and has to fight in the Texas Revolution and the Alamo. Along the way, he experiences great friendships and great loss. Through reading this fictional book, students will get to learn about Mexico and its complicated history with the United States.

books.title: Eiffel's Tower for Young People
books.author: Rebecca Steffof
books.rationale: Set in the late 1800s, the book follows engineer Gustave Eiffel and his team as they design and construct the tower for the 1889 Paris World’s Fair, a celebration of modern industry and innovation. The book places the Eiffel Tower in a global context, helping students see how world’s fairs, new technologies, and urban growth reflected broader historical shifts that were affecting many countries at the time. Students will also be introduced to other famous figures of that time including Annie Oakley, Dinah Salifou (king of Nalu people of Guinea), the Shah of Persia and the Van Gogh brothers. Architecture will also be integrated into the lesson plans.

books.title: Historical Atlas of the World 6th Ed.
books.author: Rand McNally
books.rationale: Presents important periods and turning points in 5,000 years of history. Thematic maps include literacy, languages, religions, and more. Used across curriculum.
books.sharedText:
Resources: Grades 4-6
Resources: Grades 7-8
Resources: Grade 9
Resources: Grade 10
Resources: Grade 11
Resources: Grade 12
Story of Arithmetic: Part 1
Story of Arithmetic: Part 2
Story of Geometry: Part 1
Plutarch: Grades 6-8
First Citizen Stories
Ancient History: Grades 7-9

books.title: A Short History of Ancient Rome
books.author: Pascal Hughes
books.rationale: This book charts the dramatic twists and turns of the Roman kingdom, the republic, the empire and its decline. Pascal Hughes writes the history in a popular form that is engaging and easy to understand. Students will walk away from the reading with a deep understanding of one of the most powerful nations in the world.
books.noteText: The first few chapters contain sensitive content and will not be assigned to students. Next year's ancient history rotation is 100 B.C.-350 A.D. Therefore, students will begin with ch.6 when Sulla becomes dictator.

books.title: The Witness of Early Christian Women: Mothers of the Church
books.author: Mike Aquilina
books.rationale: Source documents/writings alongside short biographies of women and their impact in the early church.

books.title: Jeremy's War 1812
books.author: John Ibbitson
books.rationale: A gripping account of the War of 1812 told from the perspective of Jeremy Fields, a fifteen-year-old orphaned boy who joins a regiment of British soldiers and eventually becomes the personal servant of Sir Isaac Brock. Historical details blend smoothly with a fictional story to create a detailed recreation of this definitive period in Canada's pre-Confederation history.

books.title: Sophie's Rebellion
books.author: Beverley Boissery
books.rationale: Sophie's Rebellion is a fictional account of one American girl's experience of the 1837-1838 Rebellions, as she is held prisoner alongside her British travelling companions and befriends a young rebel named Luc. Through Sophie's eyes, the reader is able to see multiple perspectives of this conflict and is introduced to a number of notable historical figures. A trained historian, Beverley Boissery creates an authentically detailed account of Upper and Lower Canada in the mid-nineteenth century.
books.sharedText:
Canadian History: Grades 9-10
Canadian History: Grades 7-8

books.title: The Story of Canada
books.author: Janet Lunn, Christopher Moore
books.rationale: Canadian history is told through rich narrative, recreations of daily life, folk tales, and fascinating facts.
books.maySubText: previous edition. Page numbers will differ from lesson plans.
books.scopeText:
Term 1 (Partial Term)
Term 2 (Partial Term)
Term 3 (Partial Term)
books.sharedText:
Canadian History: Grades 5-6
Canadian History: Grades 7-8

books.title: Elijah of Buxton
books.author: Christopher Paul Curtis
books.rationale: This multi-award-winning novel brings to life the real town of Buxton, Ontario, a settlement of runaway slaves and free Black people that was a haven of safety and hope, especially in the years leading up to the Civil War. The story is told through the distinctive voice of Elijah, the first free-born child of Buxton, who is equal parts sensitive, naive, earnest, playful, and mischievous. When he is forced to discover the horrors of slavery first-hand, he demonstrates courage and maturity beyond his young age. Christopher Paul Curtis handles this difficult topic deftly, blending gentle humour and wisdom with a gripping plot of risk and danger.

books.title: The Charlottetown Conference and the Birth of Confederation
books.author: Deirdre Kessler
books.rationale: The Charlottetown Conference and the Birth of Confederation is a detailed, engagingly-written account of the historical factors, issues, political players, and conferences which eventually led to Canada's Confederation in 1867. Attention is paid to the motives and pressures urging British North America towards unity, as well as the specific interests and context of each of the colonies before Confederation. The book brings this historical period to life with numerous photographs, paintings, archival documents, and other primary sources.
books.sharedText:
Canadian History: Grades 7-8
Canadian History: Grades 9-10

Along the way, he experiences great friendships and great loss. Through reading this fictional book, students will get to learn about Mexico and its complicated history with the United States.

books.title: A Short History of Ancient Rome
books.author: Pascal Hughes
books.rationale: This book charts the dramatic twists and turns of the Roman kingdom, the republic, the empire and its decline. Pascal Hughes writes the history in a popular form that is engaging and easy to understand. Students will walk away from the reading with a deep understanding of one of the most powerful nations in the world.
books.noteText: The first few chapters contain sensitive content and will not be assigned to students. Next year's ancient history rotation is 100 B.C.-350 A.D. Therefore, students will begin with ch.6 when Sulla becomes dictator.

books.title: The Witness of Early Christian Women: Mothers of the Church
books.author: Mike Aquilina
books.rationale: Source documents/writings alongside short biographies of women and their impact in the early church.

books.title: Reading American Horizons
books.author: Various Authors
books.rationale: From sources like Andrew Jackson’s “Veto of the Bank of the United States" to “the Declaration of Sentiments,” students will get their hands on written and visual documents that will help them see firsthand what people in the 1800s were encountering on a day-to-day basis. Students will develop their historical thinking skills by using these primary sources.

books.title: The Narrative of Lunsford Lane, Formerly of Raleigh, N. C.
books.author: Lunsford Lane
books.rationale: Originally published in 1842, this autobiography of Lunsford Lane details his life of slavery and his eventual escape using a unique method, purchasing his own freedom. To do this, he would need to have help from others around him. Lunsford Lane faced many hardships and took on entire judicial systems in his quest for his and his family’s freedom. Students, when reading The Narrative of Lunsford Lane, will encounter the real-life story of a brave man who lost his home but freed himself and his family.

books.title: American History, Volume 1: 1492-1877
books.author: Thomas Kidd
books.rationale: In American History: Volumes 1 and 2, Thomas Kidd tells the story of the United States from early American encounters to the modern-day American culture in an engaging and fresh way. Thomas Kidd uses primary sources and other scholarly resources to introduce the student to the people and places that have great historical significance—whether good or bad.

books.title: They Fought Like Demons: Women Soldiers in the Civil War
books.author: De Anne Blanton & Lauren M. Cook
books.rationale: Most people will never know the truth behind people like Albert Cashier, Frances Clayton, or the countless other aliases that women took on so that they could join the fight for causes that they believed in. The authors dive into military archives with the goal to uncover the many stories of women who stepped into the role of a male soldier in the 1800s. And with engaging chapters and well-rounded research, the student will be able to see the war through the eyes of women as they took to the battlefield alongside brothers, sons, fathers, and husbands. These women would come to be known later on—courtesy of documents from soldiers—as soldiers who fought like demons.

books.title: American History, Volume 2: 1877 - Present
books.author: Thomas Kidd
books.rationale: In American History: Volumes 1 and 2, Thomas Kidd tells the story of the United States from early American encounters to the modern-day American culture in an engaging and fresh way. Thomas Kidd uses primary sources and other scholarly resources to introduce the student to the people and places that have great historical significance—whether good or bad.

books.title: I Am a Man: Chief Standing Bear's Journey for Justice
books.author: Joe Starita
books.rationale: This book is about Chief Standing Bear’s fight for justice as he navigates the loss of his land—taken by manipulation and coercion. Joe Starita uses primary sources to help the audience see many of the hardships that Chief Standing Bear went through — like the loss of his daughter and brother —and how others came to fight for him and his people. It grounds the reader in the time and experiences of another people group who were struggling to find their identity in a new nation.
books.noteText: Available on Learning Ally with Audio.

books.title: A Little Book for New Historians
books.author: Robert Tracy McKenzie
books.rationale: Robert Tracy McKenzie offers a concise, clear, and beautifully-written introduction to the study of history.  Loaded with concrete examples and insightful principles, this primer shows how the study of history, faithfully pursued, can shape your heart as well as your mind. Videos of the author discussing his book are found in the lesson plans.
books.noteText: Also highly recommended as a resource for parents and teachers.

books.title: Erebus: The Story of a Ship
books.author: Michael Palin
books.rationale: Erebus is a book about exploration and mystery. Palin takes the student into two of the most harrowing exploration trips ever recorded, the last trip being unsuccessful and leading to a mystery that still puzzles modern-day minds. By reading Erebus, students will get an account of what is known about the first ever explorations into Antarctica, the most isolated continent in the world.
books.sharedText:
World History: Grade 9
World History: Grades 10-12

books.title: Blood and Iron: The Rise and Fall of the German Empire
books.author: Katja Hoyer
books.rationale: Katja Hoyer is a German-British historian and expert in the field of Germany’s history. She brings that expertise to the pages of her book which engages with the people, policies, and places that helped build the German Empire and ultimately led to its downfall. This book offers the student the history of a nation whose national pride led to two World Wars.
books.sharedText:
World History: Grades 10-12
World History: Grade 9

books.title: Historical Atlas of the World 6th Ed.
books.author: Rand McNally
books.rationale: Presents important periods and turning points in 5,000 years of history. Thematic maps include literacy, languages, religions, and more. Used across curriculum.

books.sharedText:
Canadian History: Grades 9-10
Canadian History: Grades 7-8

books.title: The Underground Railroad: Next Stop, Toronto!
books.author: Adrienne Shadd, Afua Cooper, & Karolyn Smardz Frost
books.rationale: The Underground Railroad: Next Stop, Toronto! is a detailed account of Black history in Canada focusing on the stories of those who made the dangerous journey north to freedom. Using a wide variety of primary sources, including archival documents, newspaper articles, letters, photographs, maps, archeological artifacts, and more, this book recounts what happened to those freedom-seekers once they arrived safely and settled. Through this lens, readers are also introduced to what life was like in the city of Toronto in the nineteenth century. The presence and contribution of Black people who made their homes in Toronto contributed significantly to the history and development of the city.

books.title: Vanished Beyond the Map
books.author: Adam Shoalts
books.rationale: Vanished Beyond the Map unfolds like a mystery novel: after first stumbling upon the name Hubert Darrell in an old encyclopedia, Adam Shoalts sets off on a journey through the arctic and the archives to discover as much as he can about this nearly-forgotten explorer, mapmaker and guide who disappeared in 1910. This book is remarkable for showing readers what is involved in trying to solve a historical mystery: where do you find clues? what kinds of questions do you ask? how much can we really know about the past? But it is also a book which situates Darrell within the wider world of geographers and explorers in the late-nineteenth century, who were attempting to complete the map of the world by charting the unknown lands of the arctic and antarctic.
books.noteText: This book is assigned for both Geography: Grade 8 (Canada) and History: Grade 9-10 (Canada). Note the possible overlap if you are combining students in either of these courses.
books.sharedText:
Geography: Grade 8 (Canada)
Canadian History: Grades 9-10

books.title: Red Wolf

books.title: I Am a Man: Chief Standing Bear's Journey for Justice
books.author: Joe Starita

books.noteText: Available on Learning Ally with Audio.

books.title: When America First Met China
books.author: Eric Jay Dolin
books.rationale: This is the story of trade between nations. One nation has just been born from a bloody battle with Britain, and one nation is steeped in a 2000-year tradition. The United States of America has to break away from using its mother nation for trading, and as it navigates trade with China, there will be collisions, barriers, and failings, but there will also be triumphs. China’s culture is also explored in great depth as Dolin uses stories of real people who broke the rules and recounted all the different happenings taking place in this nation.

books.title: Erebus: The Story of a Ship
books.author: Michael Palin
books.rationale: Erebus is a book about exploration and mystery. Palin takes the student into two of the most harrowing exploration trips ever recorded, the last trip being unsuccessful and leading to a mystery that still puzzles modern-day minds. By reading Erebus, students will get an account of what is known about the first ever explorations into Antarctica, the most isolated continent in the world.

World History: Grade 9

books.title: Christians Reading Classics
books.author: Nadya Williams
books.rationale: Christianity began in a pagan land with its own myths and writings about gods, villains, and heroes. These stories would become ingrained in the culture. Stories from Homer and writings from Plato and Aristotle would impact the Greco-Roman world and still impact our world.  Williams explores the importance of understanding the content of these classics and the way they impacted the world, as Christians.
books.noteText: Only specific chapters will be assigned.

books.title: Ancient Empires: From Mesopotamia to the Rise of Islam
books.author: Eric H. Cline & Mark W. Graham
books.rationale: Ancient Empires is a relatively brief yet comprehensive and even-handed overview of the ancient Near East, the Mediterranean, and Europe, including the Greco-Roman world, Late Antiquity, and the early Muslim period.
books.noteText: Used for several rotations.

books.title: Early Christian Writings: The Apostolic Fathers edited
books.author: Andrew Louth
books.rationale: With the birth of Christianity came a lot of writings from a lot of different authors. In this collection, authors like Clement, Ignatius, Barnabas, and Polycarp contribute to the growth of Christianity and write documents against heresies, trying to answer questions of philosophy and questions about their God.

Stories from Homer and writings from Plato and Aristotle would impact the Greco-Roman world and still impact our world.  Williams explores the importance of understanding the content of these classics and the way they impacted the world, as Christians.
books.noteText: Only specific chapters will be assigned.

books.title: Ancient Empires: From Mesopotamia to the Rise of Islam
books.author: Eric H. Cline & Mark W. Graham
books.rationale: Ancient Empires is a relatively brief yet comprehensive and even-handed overview of the ancient Near East, the Mediterranean, and Europe, including the Greco-Roman world, Late Antiquity, and the early Muslim period.
books.noteText: Used for several rotations.

books.title: Early Christian Writings: The Apostolic Fathers edited
books.author: Andrew Louth
books.rationale: With the birth of Christianity came a lot of writings from a lot of different authors. In this collection, authors like Clement, Ignatius, Barnabas, and Polycarp contribute to the growth of Christianity and write documents against heresies, trying to answer questions of philosophy and questions about their God.

books.title: Making of Canada: An Epic History in Twenty Extraordinary Lives
books.author: Greg Koabel
books.rationale: In this engaging book, Greg Koabel challenges the notion that pre-Confederation Canada unfolded in a linear fashion and instead suggests that nation-building involved greater "regional, national, and human diversity" than is typically acknowledged. To illustrate this argument, Koabel introduces his readers to twenty episodes significant to Canada's history but whose major players have been largely forgotten. These biographical vignettes give readers a more personal - and memorable - connection to Canadian history.
books.noteText: This book will be used over two years in high school (Rotation 2 and Rotation 3). In 2026-2027, students will read the second half of the book. Interested students may wish to read the first half over the summer, but it is not necessary since all chapters are self-contained.

books.title: A History of Canada in Ten Maps: Epic Stories of Charting a Mysterious Land
books.author: Adam Shoalts
books.rationale: This book tells the story of the discovery and development of the land that would become Canada by exploring the creation and usage of ten significant maps. It is an innovative and engaging approach to Canadian history, written by an academic and modern-day adventurer who has followed many of the routes taken by the explorers he includes in this book. It shows how history writing can create a more vivid representation of the past when it draws on biography, story-telling, geography, and cartography.
books.noteText: This book will be used over two years in high school (Rotation 2 and Rotation 3). In 2026-2027, students will read the second half of the book. Interested students may wish to read the first half over the summer, but it is not necessary since all chapters are mostly self-contained.

books.title: Early Voices
books.author: Mary Alice Downie
books.rationale: This collection of writings by 29 different women presents a kaleidoscope image of Canada's history. The wide range of individual voices, backgrounds, and histories provided by these primary documents complement the other historical non-fiction and fiction read during the year.
books.noteText: This book will be used over multiple years during high school

books.title: How Different It Was: Canadians at the Time of Confederation
books.author: Michael J. Goodspeed
books.rationale: One of the key lessons of history is that the past was - to greater or lesser degrees - fundamentally different than the present, and this is shown clearly in Michael Goodspeed's book, How Different it Was: Canadians at the Time of Confederation. Goodspeed provides a multi-faceted account of the experiences of Canadians in the 1860s, exploring factors such as geographical differences, cultural practices, family dynamics, religious observances, scientific beliefs, leisure activities, habits, and attitudes, as well as the burgeoning nation of Canada's place in the wider nineteenth-century geopolitical arena. He draws heavily on primary source material, and the book is full of historical photographs and documents.
books.noteText: E-book edition also widely available.

books.title: The Unweaving
books.author: Cheryl Perisien
books.rationale: The Red River Resistance of 1869 (also known as the First Riel Rebellion) is brought to life through the experiences of one Métis family in this work of historical fiction. Unfolding in four perspectives, this novel explores how the turmoil and the conflict affect each member of the family differently. The narrative weaves historical events and people (including the author's own ancestor) into the lives of this one family, and it is a thoroughly researched, richly detailed, and beautifully written account of the great upheaval faced by the Red River Colony in the years after Confederation.

books.title: When America First Met China
books.author: Eric Jay Dolin
books.rationale: This is the story of trade between nations. One nation has just been born from a bloody battle with Britain, and one nation is steeped in a 2000-year tradition. The United States of America has to break away from using its mother nation for trading, and as it navigates trade with China, there will be collisions, barriers, and failings, but there will also be triumphs. China’s culture is also explored in great depth as Dolin uses stories of real people who broke the rules and recounted all the different happenings taking place in this nation.

books.title: Erebus: The Story of a Ship
books.author: Michael Palin
books.rationale: Erebus is a book about exploration and mystery. Palin takes the student into two of the most harrowing exploration trips ever recorded, the last trip being unsuccessful and leading to a mystery that still puzzles modern-day minds. By reading Erebus, students will get an account of what is known about the first ever explorations into Antarctica, the most isolated continent in the world.
books.sharedText:
World History: Grade 9
World History: Grades 10-12

books.title: Blood and Iron: The Rise and Fall of the German Empire
books.author: Katja Hoyer
books.rationale: Katja Hoyer is a German-British historian and expert in the field of Germany’s history. She brings that expertise to the pages of her book which engages with the people, policies, and places that helped build the German Empire and ultimately led to its downfall. This book offers the student the history of a nation whose national pride led to two World Wars.
books.sharedText:
World History: Grades 10-12
World History: Grade 9

books.title: Christians Reading Classics
books.author: Nadya Williams
books.rationale: Christianity began in a pagan land with its own myths and writings about gods, villains, and heroes. These stories would become ingrained in the culture. Stories from Homer and writings from Plato and Aristotle would impact the Greco-Roman world and still impact our world.  Williams explores the importance of understanding the content of these classics and the way they impacted the world, as Christians.
books.noteText: Only specific chapters will be assigned.

books.title: Ancient Empires: From Mesopotamia to the Rise of Islam
books.author: Eric H. Cline & Mark W. Graham
books.rationale: Ancient Empires is a relatively brief yet comprehensive and even-handed overview of the ancient Near East, the Mediterranean, and Europe, including the Greco-Roman world, Late Antiquity, and the early Muslim period.
books.noteText: Used for several rotations.

books.title: Early Christian Writings: The Apostolic Fathers edited
books.author: Andrew Louth
books.rationale: With the birth of Christianity came a lot of writings from a lot of different authors. In this collection, authors like Clement, Ignatius, Barnabas, and Polycarp contribute to the growth of Christianity and write documents against heresies, trying to answer questions of philosophy and questions about their God.
